title: Spotify Streaming Behavior
text:
Research Question
What drives Spotify streaming behavior?

Which songs are most popular?
Which songs create the strongest engagement?
When are listeners most active?
What can marketers learn from streaming data?

title: What Drives Spotify Streaming Behavior?
text:
Why This Matters
Streaming is not only music consumption.
It is also behavioral data.
For artists, labels, and platforms, listening data can help answer:

Which songs should be promoted?
When should campaigns be launched?
Which songs create deeper engagement?
How do fans behave after releases?

title: Does New Music Drive Fan Engagement?
text:
Dataset Overview



27,005

Streams


308

Tracks


1348.3

Listening Hours

Key variables

Track name
Album name
Release year
Listening duration
Day and hour of listening

title: What Drives Spotify Streaming Behavior?
text: Top Artists

title: Spotify Streaming Behavior
text:
Most Streamed Songs

Insight: Stream count reflects popularity and repeat listening.

title: Spotify Streaming Behavior
text:
Engagement: Listening Hours

Insight: Listening hours measure depth of engagement.

title: Spotify Streaming Behavior
text:
Popularity vs. Engagement


Popularity
Measured by:

Streams

Shows how often listeners return to a song.

Engagement
Measured by:

Listening Time

Shows how much attention a song earns.

Key idea
The most popular songs are not always the most engaging songs.

title: What Drives Spotify Streaming Behavior?
text: Listening by Day of Week

title: What Drives Spotify Streaming Behavior?
text:
Listening by Hour

Marketing Insight
If listening activity peaks at predictable hours, promotional content should be timed around those moments.

title: What Drives Spotify Streaming Behavior?
text: Album Performance

title: What Drives Spotify Streaming Behavior?
text: Average Listening Duration

title: What Drives Spotify Streaming Behavior?
text:
Marketing Insight 1
New releases can drive repeat listening
Songs with high stream counts suggest concentrated fan attention.
Recommendation:

Push promotional content shortly after release
Use short-form video and playlist placement
Encourage repeat listening through fan campaigns

title: What Drives Spotify Streaming Behavior?
text:
Marketing Insight 2
Engagement is different from popularity
Total listening hours reveal deeper engagement.
Recommendation:

Promote songs with long listening duration
Use high-engagement tracks in playlists
Consider these songs for live performances or fan content

title: What Drives Spotify Streaming Behavior?
text:
Marketing Insight 3
Timing matters
Listening behavior varies by day and hour.
Recommendation:

Schedule announcements during high-activity windows
Release reminders when listeners are already active
Use time-based targeting for social media campaigns

title: What Drives Spotify Streaming Behavior?
text:
Business Implications
For music marketers, streaming data can support:

Release timing decisions
Playlist strategy
Fan engagement campaigns
Album promotion
Content scheduling

The main value is turning listening behavior into marketing action.

title: What Drives Spotify Streaming Behavior?
text:
Limitations
This dataset is anonymized and focused on Stray Kids streaming behavior.
Limitations include:

No demographic information
No user-level identity
Limited time window
Cannot directly measure why listeners choose a song

Future analysis could include:

Longer time periods
Multiple artists
Playlist source data
User demographic segments

title: Do Steam Discounts Increase Player Engagement?
text:
Conclusion
Key Takeaway
Steam discounts appear to meaningfully increase player engagement in the short run.
Matched DID helps separate the discount effect from pre-existing game popularity.
Final point
Discounts should be evaluated not only by revenue impact, but also by their ability to reactivate players and increase platform engagement.

title: Do Steam Discounts Increase Player Engagement?
text: Thank You

title: Does New Music Drive Fan Engagement?
text:
Business Problem
Should music labels focus on new releases or older catalog songs?
Music marketers have limited promotional resources.
They need to decide whether to:

Push newly released songs
Continue promoting older catalog tracks
Balance both strategies

Research Question
Do new releases drive disproportionate listener engagement?

title: Does New Music Drive Fan Engagement?
text:
Hypothesis
H1: New releases generate higher engagement.
Why this might happen:

Fans are more excited after new music drops
New songs receive more social media attention
Streaming campaigns often focus on recent releases
New releases may create repeated listening behavior

Test
Compare new releases with catalog music.

title: Does New Music Drive Fan Engagement?
text:
New Releases vs. Catalog Music

Insight
This shows whether newer music receives more total streaming activity than older catalog songs.

title: Does New Music Drive Fan Engagement?
text:
Engagement by Listening Hours

Insight
Listening hours measure deeper engagement, not just repeated plays.

title: Does New Music Drive Fan Engagement?
text:
Disproportionate Attention

Key idea
If new releases represent a smaller share of songs but a larger share of streams or hours, then listener attention is disproportionately concentrated on new music.

title: Does New Music Drive Fan Engagement?
text:
Attention Concentration

Marketing insight: Fan attention is not evenly distributed across songs. A small number of tracks drive a large share of streams.

title: Does New Music Drive Fan Engagement?
text:
Top Tracks as Demand Signals

Marketing meaning
Top tracks act as demand signals.
They show where fans are already concentrating attention.

title: Does New Music Drive Fan Engagement?
text:
Marketing Recommendation 1
Prioritize new releases
If new releases receive a larger share of listening activity, labels should concentrate promotional effort around release windows.
Recommended actions:

Strong first-week campaigns
Playlist pitching
Short-form video promotion
Fan streaming events

title: Does New Music Drive Fan Engagement?
text:
Marketing Recommendation 2
Do not promote every song equally
Attention is usually concentrated among a small number of songs.
Recommended actions:

Identify early high-performing tracks
Shift budget toward songs with strong demand signals
Use weaker tracks for album depth, not primary promotion

title: Does New Music Drive Fan Engagement?
text:
Marketing Recommendation 3
Keep catalog music as long-term support
Older songs may still matter, but their role is different.
Catalog music can support:

Long-term fan retention
Discovery by new listeners
Playlist stability
Artist identity and nostalgia

title: Does New Music Drive Fan Engagement?
text:
Strategic Conclusion
Main finding
New music is likely to drive stronger short-term fan engagement, while catalog music supports long-term listening.
Business implication
Music marketers should use a two-part strategy:

Focus most promotional resources on new releases.
Maintain selective catalog promotion for long-term engagement.

title: Does New Music Drive Fan Engagement?
text:
Final Takeaway
Streaming data turns fan behavior into marketing strategy.
The key question is not only:
What do fans listen to?
The deeper question is:
Where should marketers invest attention and budget?

title: Do Steam Discounts Increase Player Engagement?
text:
Business Question
Do Steam discounts increase player engagement?
Steam discounts are a major marketing tool.
The key question is:
Do discounts actually increase player activity, or are discounted games already more popular?

title: Does New Music Drive Fan Engagement?
text:
Hypothesis and Model
Hypothesis
Newer songs generate higher engagement.
Engagement is measured at the song level using total listening hours.
Model
\[
\log(1 + Engagement_i) =
\beta_0 + \beta_1 SongAge_i + \beta_2 AlbumType_i + \epsilon_i
\]
Where:

Engagement = total listening hours per song
Song Age = 2025 minus release year
Album Type controls for single, album, mixtape, etc.

title: Does New Music Drive Fan Engagement?
text:
Descriptive Evidence

Initial pattern: Newer music appears to capture a large share of listener attention.

title: Does New Music Drive Fan Engagement?
text:
Regression Results

Model Output

Song age coefficient: -0.199
p-value: 0
R-squared: 0.494

Interpretation: A negative coefficient means older songs tend to receive lower engagement.

title: Do Steam Discounts Increase Player Engagement?
text:
Marketing Implications
Recommendation 1
Use discounts as an engagement tool, not only a sales tool.
Recommendation 2
Prioritize discounts for games with an existing player base that can be reactivated.
Recommendation 3
Coordinate discounts with marketing campaigns, content updates, or community events to maximize engagement.

title: Does Premium+ Drive Higher Engagement?
text:
Method: PSM + DID
Step 1: Propensity Score Matching
Premium+ users are matched with similar non-Premium+ users based on:

Age
Gender
Country
Monthly spend
Primary device
Household size

Step 2: Difference-in-Differences
After matching, I compare engagement before vs. after subscription start.
\[
Engagement_{it} = \beta_0 + \beta_1 PremiumPlus_i +
\beta_2 Post_t + \beta_3(PremiumPlus_i \times Post_t) + \epsilon_{it}
\]
Key estimate: \(\beta_3\)

title: Do Steam Discounts Increase Player Engagement?
text:
Matched Sample



11

Matched pairs


22

Games


29

Days

title: Does Premium+ Drive Higher Engagement?
text:
Matching Quality

Interpretation
After matching, Premium+ and control users should have more similar predicted probabilities of choosing Premium+.

title: Do Steam Discounts Increase Player Engagement?
text:
DID Result

Estimated Effect

Raw DID effect: 402.2 players
Regression DID effect: 64.6%
p-value: <0.001

title: Does Premium+ Drive Higher Engagement?
text:
Interpretation
If the DID estimate is positive:

Premium+ users increased engagement more than similar non-Premium+ users after subscription start.

If the DID estimate is small or insignificant:

Premium+ may not meaningfully increase engagement after accounting for selection bias.

Important caveat
This is observational data, not a randomized experiment.
PSM + DID reduces selection bias, but it does not prove causality unless the parallel trends assumption is credible.

title: Does Premium+ Drive Higher Engagement?
text:
Marketing Recommendation
If Premium+ effect is positive

Promote Premium+ to users similar to the matched high-potential group
Emphasize features that increase viewing time
Use targeted upgrade campaigns

If Premium+ effect is weak

Premium+ may be monetizing already-engaged users rather than creating new engagement
Focus on improving product features before pushing upgrades
Use retention campaigns for users with declining watch behavior

title: Do Steam Discounts Increase Player Engagement?
text:
Empirical Strategy
Matched Difference-in-Differences
I compare discounted games with similar non-discounted games.


Treatment
Games discounted on 2019-06-25

Control
Similar games not discounted during the same window

Games are matched based on pre-discount average player count.

title: Do Steam Discounts Increase Player Engagement?
text:
DID Design
\[
\log(Players_{it}) =
\beta_0 +
\beta_1 Discounted_i +
\beta_2 Post_t +
\beta_3(Discounted_i \times Post_t)
+ \epsilon_{it}
\]
Key estimate
\(\beta_3\) measures the additional change in player engagement for discounted games after the discount event.
The model includes:

Matched-pair fixed effects
Relative-day fixed effects

title: Do Steam Discounts Increase Player Engagement?
text: Parallel Trends Check

title: Do Steam Discounts Increase Player Engagement?
text:
Interpretation
Main finding
Discounted games experienced a significantly larger increase in player activity than comparable non-discounted games.
Evidence

Player activity increased for discounted games after the discount event.
Player activity declined for the matched control games.
The regression DID estimate is 64.6% with p-value <0.001.

Conclusion
The results suggest that Steam discounts are associated with a substantial increase in player engagement.

title: Do Steam Discounts Increase Player Engagement?
text:
Matched Sample Check



11

Discounted games


11

Control games


11

Matched pairs


29

Days

title: Do Steam Discounts Increase Player Engagement?
text:
Conclusion
Key Takeaway
Steam discounts appear to meaningfully increase player engagement in the short run.
Matched DID helps separate the discount effect from pre-existing game popularity.
Final point
Discounts should be evaluated not only by revenue impact, but also by their ability to reactivate players and increase platform engagement.
